practice_name: Alternate Wetting and Drying (AWD)
AWD using field water-tube monitoring (measured thresholds)
AWD with fewer flooding days (reduced continuous inundation)
Mid-season drainage (MSD)
Early-season drainage (shorten initial flooding period)
Pre-harvest drainage (timely drainage before harvest)
Intermittent irrigation scheduling (fixed wet–dry cycles)
Improved drainage outlets (faster drawdown)
Bund repair + leveling to enable controlled AWD (enables water control)
Laser land leveling (supports uniform AWD / less waterlogging)
Raised beds / furrow-irrigated rice (where applicable)
Aerobic rice management (non-flooded rice system)
Conversion of long-duration flooded rice to shorter-duration rice (less flooding time)
Use of short-duration varieties to reduce flooded period
Shift from transplanted puddled rice to systems reducing puddling/flooding duration (where defined)
Direct-Seeded Rice (DSR) (reduces flooded nursery/puddling period)
Dry seeding (non-puddled establishment)
Reduced puddling intensity (where permitted/defined)
Strip till / reduced till rice establishment (where feasible)
Controlled traffic / minimized soil disturbance (site-dependent)
Improved field channels for precise irrigation (enables AWD)
Timely weeding to maintain aerobic periods (supports AWD feasibility)
Mechanized operations reducing repeated puddling passes (site-dependent)
Optimized N rate (reduce excess N)
Split N application (better timing reduces losses)
Deep placement of urea (UDP)
Controlled-release urea (CRU)
Nitrification inhibitors (where allowed)
Urease inhibitors (where allowed)
Site-Specific Nutrient Management (SSNM)
Leaf Color Chart (LCC)-guided N dosing
Basal N reduction with more top-dressing precision
Balanced fertilization (adequate P/K to reduce excess N needs)
Avoid N application just before heavy irrigation/rain (timing rule)
Replace part of urea with organic sources under planned rates (avoid over-application)
Precision fertilizer application (band placement, calibrated applicators)
Improved manure management timing (avoid anaerobic + N surplus conditions)
Inclusion of legumes in rotation where feasible (reduces synthetic N demand)
No open-field burning of rice straw (avoided emissions)
Straw removal + off-field productive use (with leakage accounted)
Straw composting (instead of burning)
Straw incorporation with drainage/timing to reduce methane spikes (managed incorporation)
Straw mulching under non-flooded/managed water (method-dependent)
Use of straw for bioenergy with accounting (project/method dependent)
Collection/baling systems to prevent burning (enabler for #39)
Biochar application (rice husk/straw biochar)
Compost application (stabilized organic matter)
Farmyard manure application under monitored rates (SOC improvement; method-dependent)
Green manuring (e.g., Sesbania/dhaincha) with proper water management (SOC + N dynamics)
Cover crop/legume in rice fallow (where feasible) to build SOC (rotation-based crediting)
